repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 records ThmSmith/PartialID as empty seed, and the withdrawn same-qid angle-0 generic drift-LP tightening is a different kernel

published_prior_art.cite: YinLiuGengLuo2020: observed-data criteria can exclude surrogate paradox, and failure admits observationally equivalent paradox laws (https://ideas.repec.org/a/bla/scjsta/v47y2020i1p84-103.html); HsiaoTianParast2025 is a missed close comparator on surrogate-paradox resilience with historical endpoint data and new surrogate-only studies (https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.12194)

repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 has no partial-ID surrogate/noisy-proxy entry, and other visible pid bank entries target dose-response IV, dynamic compliance, or network exposure

published_prior_art.cite: HuiWalter1980: two tests in two populations with invariant conditionally independent error rates identify test properties and latent prevalences without a gold standard (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7370371/); FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2021 and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 cover generic discrete measurement-error/partial-ID computation (https://proceedings.mlr.press/v161/finkelstein21b.html; https://ideas.repec.org/a/taf/jnlasa/v119y2024i547p1778-1793.html)

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The two-proxy determinant part is too close to the uncited Hui-Walter two-test/two-population latent-class diagnostic-test model; tier=letter below novelty_target=field unless the proposal isolates a genuinely new bridge-and-drift transport theorem beyond that known measurement identification algebra.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The surrogate-paradox/resilience literature is missing Hsiao, Tian, and Parast 2025, a closer comparator for historical endpoint data plus a new surrogate-only study under deviations from transportability.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The witness sentence says the opposite-sign worlds have the same measurement kernels and differ only in latent terminal labeling; with fixed monotone binary kernels the latent marginal contrast is constrained by observed means, so the statement should say whether kernels are exactly shared or only share the same monotone-overlap class.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Assumption bridge
soundness_flags.one_line: The bridge response set is written as M_B(P) although it is defined by compatibility with P^B and later as B(P^B,q); the notation should be unified before Stage 0.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Hui-Walter-style diagnostic latent-class prior art explicitly, then state the new result as an endpoint bridge/drift sharpness theorem conditional on the already-known two-test measurement identification algebra.

Replace the Conjecture 2 flagship with a closed-form sharp bridge-and-drift transport bound whose nontrivial step is not the determinant formula but the common-kernel coupling with P^B and bounded residuals.

Add a boundary corollary where Hui-Walter measurement identification holds but separate bridge-margin-minus-drift arithmetic gives the wrong sign, proving the joint compatibility constraint is doing real work.

For Conjecture 1, give an explicit finite-K construction with numeric proxy means, bridge law, and residual budgets so the sign-ambiguity claim is not just a qualitative surrogate-paradox restatement.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around Hui-Walter and Hsiao-Parast, then sharpen the flagship to a genuinely new joint bridge/drift transport boundary rather than the known two-proxy latent-class determinant.
